Scaffold the WXT Chrome extension project with all dependencies, configuration, four entrypoints, the Zod v4 preference profile schema, WXT typed storage, the weight redistribution algorithm, and test infrastructure.

Purpose: Establish the complete project foundation that all subsequent plans build on -- schema, storage, styling, and build pipeline.
Output: A buildable WXT extension with working entrypoints, validated profile schema, storage layer, and passing test suite.

<tasks>

<task type="auto">
  <name>Task 1: Initialize WXT project with React, Tailwind, shadcn/ui, and all entrypoints</name>
  <files>
    <!-- Generated by tooling (WXT init, shadcn CLI, Tailwind init) -- minimal manual attention needed -->
    extension/package.json
    extension/tsconfig.json
    extension/postcss.config.js
    extension/components.json
    extension/src/public/icon-16.png
    extension/src/public/icon-32.png
    extension/src/public/icon-48.png
    extension/src/public/icon-128.png
    <!-- Authored: config & styling -- these need careful setup -->
    extension/wxt.config.ts
    extension/tailwind.config.js
    extension/vitest.config.ts
    extension/src/assets/styles/globals.css
    extension/src/lib/utils.ts
    extension/src/lib/theme.ts
    <!-- Authored: entrypoints -- these are the core scaffolds -->
    extension/src/entrypoints/background.ts
    extension/src/entrypoints/content.ts
    extension/src/entrypoints/popup/index.html
    extension/src/entrypoints/popup/main.tsx
    extension/src/entrypoints/popup/App.tsx
    extension/src/entrypoints/onboarding/index.html
    extension/src/entrypoints/onboarding/main.tsx
    extension/src/entrypoints/onboarding/App.tsx
  </files>
  <action>
    **Step 1 -- Initialize WXT project:**
    ```bash
    cd /Users/maximgusev/workspace/gen-ai-hackathon
    pnpm dlx wxt@latest init extension --template react --pm pnpm
    ```

    **Step 2 -- Install all dependencies (run from extension/):**
    ```bash
    cd extension
    # React Hook Form + Zod resolver
    pnpm add react-hook-form @hookform/resolvers zod

    # Tailwind CSS v3 + PostCSS (NOT v4 -- v3 is battle-tested with WXT)
    pnpm add -D tailwindcss@3 postcss autoprefixer
    pnpm dlx tailwindcss@3 init -p

    # shadcn/ui dependencies
    pnpm add tailwindcss-animate class-variance-authority clsx tailwind-merge lucide-react

    # Test infrastructure
    pnpm add -D vitest @testing-library/react @testing-library/jest-dom jsdom
    ```

    **Step 3 -- Configure tailwind.config.js:**
    Set content paths to `"./src/**/*.{html,js,ts,jsx,tsx}"`. Add the Homegate accent color `homegate: '#E4006E'` to theme.extend.colors. Enable `darkMode: 'class'`. Add `tailwindcss-animate` to plugins. Reference RESEARCH.md Pattern 6 for dark mode class approach.

    **Step 4 -- Configure tsconfig.json:**
    Add `"baseUrl": "."` and `"paths": { "@/*": ["./src/*"] }` to compilerOptions (required for shadcn/ui CLI path resolution -- see RESEARCH.md Pitfall 1). This works alongside WXT's own aliases.

    **Step 5 -- Initialize shadcn/ui:**
    ```bash
    pnpm dlx shadcn@latest init
    ```
    When prompted: style=default, baseColor=neutral, cssVariables=yes, tailwindConfig=tailwind.config.js, componentsAlias=@/components, utilsAlias=@/lib/utils.

    Then install all needed components:
    ```bash
    pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add button input label select checkbox slider card form separator switch badge progress textarea scroll-area tooltip radio-group
    ```

    **Step 6 -- Create globals.css** at `src/assets/styles/globals.css`:
    Include Tailwind directives (`@tailwind base; @tailwind components; @tailwind utilities;`) plus shadcn/ui CSS custom properties for the warm/friendly theme. Set the Homegate accent color (#E4006E) as the primary color. Include both light and dark mode CSS variable sets. Ensure warm, rounded aesthetic -- soft shadows, generous border-radius.

    **Step 7 -- Create cn() utility** at `src/lib/utils.ts`:
    Standard shadcn `cn()` function using `clsx` + `tailwind-merge`.

    **Step 8 -- Create theme manager** at `src/lib/theme.ts`:
    Implement `initTheme()` and `toggleTheme()` functions using WXT `storage.defineItem` for theme persistence (`local:theme` with fallback 'light'). Toggle `.dark` class on `document.documentElement`. Follow RESEARCH.md Pattern 6 exactly.

    **Step 9 -- Configure wxt.config.ts:**
    ```typescript
    import { defineConfig } from 'wxt';
    export default defineConfig({
      srcDir: 'src',
      extensionApi: 'chrome',
      modules: ['@wxt-dev/module-react'],
      manifest: {
        name: 'HomeMatch',
        description: 'AI-powered property match scoring for Homegate.ch',
        version: '0.1.0',
        permissions: ['storage'],
        host_permissions: ['*://*.homegate.ch/*'],
      },
    });
    ```

    **Step 10 -- Create entrypoints:**

    `src/entrypoints/background.ts`: Service worker with `browser.runtime.onInstalled` listener. On `reason === 'install'`, open onboarding page via `browser.tabs.create({ url: browser.runtime.getURL('/onboarding.html') })`. Follow RESEARCH.md Pattern 1. Register listener synchronously at top level (MV3 requirement -- Pitfall 2).

    `src/entrypoints/content.ts`: Placeholder content script matching `*://*.homegate.ch/*`. Just logs "[HomeMatch] Content script loaded". Follow RESEARCH.md Content Script Placeholder example.

    `src/entrypoints/popup/`: Create index.html (basic HTML shell with #root div), main.tsx (React 18 createRoot, import globals.css, call initTheme()), and App.tsx (placeholder "HomeMatch" heading -- will be replaced in Plan 04).

    `src/entrypoints/onboarding/`: Create index.html (basic HTML shell with #root div, title "HomeMatch - Setup"), main.tsx (React 18 createRoot, import globals.css, call initTheme()), and App.tsx (placeholder "Onboarding" heading -- will be replaced in Plan 04).

    **Step 11 -- Create placeholder icon PNGs** at src/public/ (16x16, 32x32, 48x48, 128x128). Generate simple colored squares or use a basic SVG-to-PNG approach. The Homegate magenta color (#E4006E) as background with "HM" text is sufficient for development.

    **Step 12 -- Create vitest.config.ts:**
    Configure Vitest with jsdom environment and WXT's Vitest plugin if available. Set test include pattern to `src/__tests__/**/*.test.ts`. Add path aliases matching tsconfig (@/ -> ./src/).

    **Step 13 -- Verify the build:**
    ```bash
    pnpm build
    ```
    Extension should build without errors. Check that `.output/chrome-mv3/manifest.json` exists and contains all four entrypoints.
  </action>
  <verify>
    <automated>cd /Users/maximgusev/workspace/gen-ai-hackathon/extension && pnpm build 2>&1 | tail -20</automated>
  </verify>
  <done>
    WXT extension builds successfully. manifest.json contains background, content_scripts, action (popup), and web_accessible_resources. All config files exist. shadcn/ui components installed. Tailwind configured with Homegate accent color and dark mode.
  </done>
</task>

<task type="auto" tdd="true">
  <name>Task 2: Create profile schema, storage layer, weight algorithm, background test, and full test suite</name>
  <files>
    extension/src/schema/profile.ts
    extension/src/schema/weights.ts
    extension/src/storage/profile-storage.ts
    extension/src/utils/weight-redistribution.ts
    extension/src/__tests__/profile-schema.test.ts
    extension/src/__tests__/weight-redistribution.test.ts
    extension/src/__tests__/profile-storage.test.ts
    extension/src/__tests__/background.test.ts
  </files>
  <behavior>
    Profile Schema (profile-schema.test.ts):
    - Valid complete profile passes validation (all fields populated)
    - Valid minimal profile passes validation (only schemaVersion + timestamps)
    - All fields are optional except schemaVersion, createdAt, updatedAt
    - listingType accepts only 'rent' or 'buy'
    - priceMin/priceMax must be >= 0 when provided
    - roomsMin/roomsMax must be >= 0 when provided
    - livingSpaceMin/livingSpaceMax must be >= 0 when provided
    - radiusKm must be 0-100 when provided
    - features is an array of strings
    - softCriteria is an array of {id, category, text, isCustom} objects
    - weights is a record of string keys to numbers 0-100
    - schemaVersion must be literal 1

    Weight Redistribution (weight-redistribution.test.ts):
    - Moving one slider redistributes others proportionally
    - All values always sum to exactly 100
    - Setting one slider to 100 sets all others to 0
    - Setting one slider to 0 distributes its share to others proportionally
    - Works with 2 categories (edge case)
    - Works with 5+ categories
    - Handles all-zero others by distributing equally
    - Values are rounded to 1 decimal place
    - Rounding correction ensures exact 100 sum

    Profile Storage (profile-storage.test.ts):
    - profileStorage.getValue() returns null when no profile saved
    - profileStorage.setValue() saves and getValue() retrieves the same profile
    - wizardStateStorage has correct fallback (currentStep: 0, completedSteps: [], partialData: {})
    - Storage version is 1

    Background onInstalled (background.test.ts):
    - onInstalled handler calls browser.tabs.create with onboarding URL when reason is 'install'
    - onInstalled handler does NOT open onboarding tab when reason is 'update'
    - onInstalled handler does NOT open onboarding tab when reason is 'chrome_update'
  </behavior>
  <action>
    **Write tests first (RED phase):**

    Create `src/__tests__/profile-schema.test.ts`: Import PreferenceProfileSchema from schema/profile. Write tests for each behavior listed above. Use Zod's `.safeParse()` to test valid/invalid inputs.

    Create `src/__tests__/weight-redistribution.test.ts`: Import redistributeWeights from utils/weight-redistribution. Write tests for each behavior listed above. Verify sums using `Object.values(result).reduce((s,v) => s+v, 0)`.

    Create `src/__tests__/profile-storage.test.ts`: Import profileStorage, wizardStateStorage from storage/profile-storage. Mock WXT storage or use fakeBrowser if available from WXT Vitest plugin. Test read/write cycle.

    Create `src/__tests__/background.test.ts`: Test the onInstalled handler from background.ts. Use WXT's `fakeBrowser` (from `wxt/testing`) to mock `browser.runtime.onInstalled` and `browser.tabs.create`. Structure:
    ```typescript
    import { fakeBrowser } from 'wxt/testing';
    import { describe, it, expect, beforeEach, vi } from 'vitest';

    // Import the background script to register its listeners
    // The exact import path depends on how WXT exports background entrypoints.
    // If direct import doesn't work, extract the onInstalled logic into a
    // named export function (e.g., `export function handleInstalled(details)`)
    // in background.ts and test that function directly.

    describe('background onInstalled', () => {
      beforeEach(() => {
        fakeBrowser.reset();
      });

      it('opens onboarding tab on first install', async () => {
        // Fire the onInstalled event with reason: 'install'
        await fakeBrowser.runtime.onInstalled.trigger({ reason: 'install' });
        // Verify browser.tabs.create was called with onboarding URL
        expect(fakeBrowser.tabs.create).toHaveBeenCalledWith(
          expect.objectContaining({ url: expect.stringContaining('onboarding.html') })
        );
      });

      it('does NOT open onboarding tab on update', async () => {
        await fakeBrowser.runtime.onInstalled.trigger({ reason: 'update' });
        expect(fakeBrowser.tabs.create).not.toHaveBeenCalled();
      });

      it('does NOT open onboarding tab on chrome_update', async () => {
        await fakeBrowser.runtime.onInstalled.trigger({ reason: 'chrome_update' });
        expect(fakeBrowser.tabs.create).not.toHaveBeenCalled();
      });
    });
    ```
    NOTE: If WXT's fakeBrowser does not support triggering onInstalled events or the import pattern doesn't work, refactor background.ts to export a testable `handleInstalled(details)` function that background.ts calls internally, and test that function directly with manual mocks of `browser.tabs.create` and `browser.runtime.getURL`.

    Run tests -- all must FAIL (schemas and functions don't exist yet).

    **Implement (GREEN phase):**

    Create `src/schema/profile.ts`: Follow RESEARCH.md Code Examples "Preference Profile Schema" exactly. Use Zod v4 patterns (NOT v3 -- see Pitfall 4). Key: `z.literal(1)` for schemaVersion, all filter fields `.optional()`, `softCriteria` with `.default([])`, `weights` with `.default({})`. Export: `PreferenceProfileSchema`, `PreferenceProfile` (inferred type), `SoftCriterionSchema`, `SoftCriterion`, `StepFiltersSchema` (subset for Step 1 form validation), `StepSoftCriteriaSchema` (subset for Step 2).

    Create `src/schema/weights.ts`: Export `WeightsSchema` as `z.record(z.string(), z.number().min(0).max(100))`. Also export a `validateWeightsSum` function that checks if values sum to 100 within tolerance of 0.5.

    Create `src/storage/profile-storage.ts`: Follow RESEARCH.md Pattern 2 exactly. Use `storage.defineItem<PreferenceProfile | null>('local:userProfile', { fallback: null, version: 1 })` for profile. Use `storage.defineItem<WizardState>('local:wizardState', { fallback: { currentStep: 0, completedSteps: [], partialData: {} }, version: 1 })` for wizard state. Export both.

    Create `src/utils/weight-redistribution.ts`: Follow RESEARCH.md Pattern 4 exactly. Implement `redistributeWeights(weights, changedKey, newValue)`. Clamp to 0-100. Handle edge case where all others are 0 (distribute equally). Proportional redistribution for normal case. Round to 1 decimal. Fix rounding error to ensure exact 100% sum. Export the function.

    Update `src/entrypoints/background.ts` (if needed): Ensure the onInstalled handler is structured so it can be tested. If fakeBrowser can trigger events on the imported module, no change needed. If not, extract the handler into a named export: `export function handleInstalled(details: { reason: string }) { ... }` and call it from the listener registration.

    Run tests -- all must PASS.
  </action>
  <verify>
    <automated>cd /Users/maximgusev/workspace/gen-ai-hackathon/extension && pnpm vitest run --reporter=verbose 2>&1 | tail -40</automated>
  </verify>
  <done>
    All tests pass including background.test.ts. Profile schema validates all 14 preference fields correctly. Weight redistribution always sums to 100%. Storage layer reads/writes profiles with version 1. onInstalled handler opens onboarding only on first install. Schema exports types consumed by wizard step forms.
  </done>
</task>

</tasks>
